marketState: neutral-bearish — extreme heat premium fading against storage surplus, weak seasonality, and converging fair value
summary: Front-month NG=F at $3.17 sits $0.21 above the fair-value midpoint of $2.96 (band $2.46–$3.46), a modest over-valuation the model projects will converge to near-zero within 21 days as fair value drifts up to meet price. Critically, the weather demand trend has reversed from the previous snapshot: demand is now 169.7 Bcf/d, down −15.1 Bcf/day in 24 hours, indicating the heat peak has likely passed — even though both the 1-7D and 8-16D windows remain above the 5-year maximum at +43% and +27% above the 5y average respectively. Structural bears dominate the medium-term picture: storage is +151 Bcf above the 5-year average after a bearish injection surprise (+76 Bcf actual vs +70 Bcf forecast), seasonality delivers only a 19% probability of a higher price in four weeks with a −1.8% typical drift and explicitly low reliability, and technicals are broadly bearish across five of six timeframes with 50-EMA below 200-EMA on 1d and 1w. The key contrarian signal remains managed-money net shorts at the 18th percentile of their 5-year range — deeply crowded positioning that could fuel a sharp short squeeze if any bullish catalyst re-emerges.

longTerm.entry: $3.20 zone (current market or bounce to $3.28–$3.35 for better entry)
longTerm.riskReward: 1:2.1 (measured to TP2); partial close at TP1 advised
longTerm.rationale: Seasonality flags only a 19% probability of a higher price in 4 weeks with a typical −1.8% drift and explicitly low reliability, while the fair-value model shows the front month is slightly over-valued now and converges to essentially fair value by day +21 — implying the current price already embeds the expected weather premium. Storage at +151 Bcf above the 5-year average is a structural bearish anchor: even extreme heat has failed to prevent a bearish injection surprise this week, confirming supply headroom. Daily and weekly technicals are both bearish (50-EMA below 200-EMA, RSI sub-50, MACD histogram negative on 1d), and the futures curve shows front/Sep backwardation resolving into pronounced shoulder-month weakness through Oct before the winter strip re-prices.
longTerm.invalidation: Daily close above $3.47 (upper fair-value band top); demand trend reverses to 3+ consecutive days of rising readings; or two consecutive weekly storage reports come in 30+ Bcf below consensus

shortTerm.rationale: Conflicting near-term signals argue for patience: both the 1-7D and 8-16D demand windows remain above the 5-year maximum, sustaining a weather bid, but the 24-hour demand trend has reversed sharply to −15.1 Bcf/day, signalling the heat peak has passed. The 1h RSI at 30.8 is approaching oversold, and MACD histograms on the 15m and 1h timeframes are turning positive — raising near-term bounce risk. MM shorts at the 18th percentile add short-squeeze risk on any bullish catalyst, making a fresh short at current levels poor risk management; a bounce into the $3.28–$3.35 zone would provide a defined entry with room to the $3.47 stop.
shortTerm.trigger: Enter short on a bounce to $3.28–$3.35 with a bearish 4h reversal candle (stop $3.48, TP1 $3.08, TP2 $2.95, approx R:R 1:1.8); alternatively, a confirmed break below $3.05 on expanding bearish 1h momentum clears the weather-bid floor for a momentum short entry

Storage +151 Bcf above 5y average with bearish injection surprise (+6 Bcf vs forecast) — structural ceiling on sustained rallies

risks: Demand trend reverses higher again — another heat dome extension could sustain the weather bid and squeeze crowded MM shorts through $3.47 upper band
